articles.headline: RSS's Silent Infiltration: Historian Tanika Sarkar on its Deep Societal Reach
articles.explainer:
• Renowned historian Tanika Sarkar highlights the silent, pervasive influence of the Rashtriya Swayamsevak Sangh (RSS) across Indian society.
• The RSS's methodical approach makes its organizational depth and reach difficult for external observers to fully gauge.
• This strategy allows the organization to embed its ideology at various grassroots levels without overt visibility.
• Concerns are raised about the long-term implications of such widespread, subtle ideological penetration on national discourse.
gsSubTopics: Role of women and women’s organization
population and associated issues
poverty and developmental issues
urbanization, their problems and their remedies
effects of globalization on Indian society
social empowerment
communalism
regionalism
secularism
prelimsPoints: Tanika Sarkar is a prominent Indian historian known for her work on gender, religion, and nationalism.
RSS is a right-wing Hindu nationalist paramilitary volunteer organization in India.
mainsPoints: Analyze the methods employed by organizations like RSS to achieve deep societal penetration.
Discuss the challenges in assessing the true extent and impact of such 'silent' organizational work.
Critically evaluate the implications of widespread ideological influence on India's secular fabric and democratic institutions.
articles.imageDescription: A blurred image depicting a large gathering, symbolizing widespread societal presence, with a subtle saffron hue.
articles.trivia: The RSS was founded in 1925 by Keshav Baliram Hedgewar.

articles.headline: Distinguishing Ideology from Religion: Why 'Hindutva is Not Hinduism' Remains a Core Debate
articles.explainer:
• The article reiterates the crucial distinction between Hindutva as a political ideology and Hinduism as a diverse religion.
• Scholars argue that Hindutva, often characterized by its exclusionary principles, cannot be equated with the inclusive and pluralistic tenets of Hinduism.
• The assertion 'No Muslim, no Hindutva' is analyzed to highlight the inherent exclusionary nature of the Hindutva ideology.
• This conceptual clarity is vital for understanding contemporary political and social discourse in India.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.
prelimsPoints: Hindutva was primarily articulated by Vinayak Damodar Savarkar in his 1923 book 'Essentials of Hindutva'.
The Supreme Court of India has, in various judgments, distinguished between Hindutva as a way of life and a political ideology.
mainsPoints: Elaborate on the historical and ideological differences between Hindutva and Hinduism.
Discuss the implications of conflating a political ideology with a religion for India's constitutional secularism.
Analyze how the 'No Muslim, no Hindutva' perspective shapes political narratives and societal divisions in India.
articles.imageDescription: A conceptual image showing two distinct thought bubbles, one representing diverse religious symbols and the other a rigid, singular political symbol.

articles.headline: The Concept of Akhand Bharat: Historical Ideal or Modern Political Tool?
articles.explainer:
• The idea of 'Akhand Bharat' (Undivided India) is explored, questioning its feasibility and relevance in contemporary geopolitics.
• Historically, Akhand Bharat refers to a unified Indian subcontinent encompassing present-day India, Pakistan, Bangladesh, Afghanistan, Nepal, Bhutan, Tibet, and Myanmar.
• The article delves into whether this historical ideal can be 'reimagined' in the 21st century or if it serves primarily as a nationalist narrative.
• It examines the challenges and implications of such a concept for regional stability and international relations.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Modern Indian history from about the middle of the eighteenth century until the present- significant events, personalities, issues.
The Freedom Struggle — its various stages and important contributors /contributions from different parts of the country.
Post-independence consolidation and reorganization within the country.
prelimsPoints: The concept of Akhand Bharat is often associated with the Hindu nationalist movement.
The idea envisions a greater India that includes territories historically or culturally linked to the Indian subcontinent.
mainsPoints: Discuss the historical origins and evolution of the concept of Akhand Bharat.
Analyze the political and cultural motivations behind advocating for Akhand Bharat in modern India.
Evaluate the geopolitical implications and challenges of reimagining Akhand Bharat in the current international order.
articles.imageDescription: An old map of the Indian subcontinent showing its historical territorial extent, overlaid with modern borders.
articles.trivia: The geographical extent of Akhand Bharat has varied in different historical and ideological interpretations.

articles.headline: Fostering Harmony: Can RSS Shed Exclusionary Ideas for India's Progress?
articles.explainer:
• Sudheendra Kulkarni argues that India's progress is intrinsically linked to fostering Hindu–Muslim harmony and healing historical wounds.
• The article critically examines whether the RSS, known for its exclusionary ideas, possesses the imagination to adapt and embrace inclusivity.
• It posits a challenge to the RSS to move beyond its current ideological framework for the greater good of national unity.
• The debate centers on whether a major societal organization can evolve its core tenets to align with a pluralistic vision of India.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of communal harmony as a prerequisite for national progress and development in India.
Critically analyze the historical stance of the RSS on Hindu-Muslim relations and its implications for national integration.
Suggest pathways through which organizations like the RSS could contribute to fostering an inclusive and harmonious society in India.
articles.imageDescription: An illustration showing hands of different faiths clasped together, symbolizing unity and harmony.
articles.trivia: Mahatma Gandhi strongly advocated for Hindu-Muslim unity as essential for India's independence and future.

articles.headline: Internal Power Dynamics: Will RSS Maintain Control Over its Most Successful Political Creation?
articles.explainer:
• The article probes the evolving relationship between the RSS and its political offshoots, particularly the ruling party.
• It raises questions about the extent to which the RSS continues to exert ideological and organizational control over its 'most successful creation'.
• Speculation suggests potential shifts in power dynamics, where the political arm might assert greater autonomy.
• This dynamic is crucial for understanding future policy directions and the ideological trajectory of the larger nationalist movement.
gsSubTopics: Pressure Groups and Formal/Informal Associations and their role in the Polity.
prelimsPoints: The Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) is widely considered the political offshoot of the RSS.
The RSS provides a significant cadre base and ideological guidance to various organizations within the Sangh Parivar.
mainsPoints: Analyze the historical relationship between the RSS and its political wings, outlining the nature of control and influence.
Discuss the factors that could lead to a potential shift in the power dynamic between the RSS and its political creation.
Evaluate the implications of such a shift for Indian politics and governance.
articles.imageDescription: An intricate diagram showing a central hub (RSS) connected to multiple spokes (political/social organizations), with some connections appearing stressed.
articles.trivia: The first Prime Minister from the BJP, Atal Bihari Vajpayee, was a lifelong member of the RSS.

articles.headline: Literary Landscape: New Books on Feminist Street Theatre and Salma's Translated Novel
articles.explainer:
• The latest literary releases highlight a fresh translation of Tamil writer Salma’s debut novel, bringing her work to a wider audience.
• Another notable book offers a richly detailed study of feminist street theatre in India, exploring its role in social commentary and activism.
• These publications underscore the vibrant and diverse literary and cultural scene in India, focusing on critical social themes.
• The books contribute to understanding gender dynamics, cultural expression, and regional literature.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Role of women and women’s organization
social empowerment
prelimsPoints: Salma is a prominent contemporary Tamil writer, poet, and activist.
Feminist street theatre often employs nukkad natak (street play) format to raise awareness on social issues.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of regional literature translations in promoting cultural exchange and understanding across India.
Analyze the role of feminist street theatre as a tool for social change and empowerment in India.
Examine how contemporary literature addresses critical social issues like gender inequality and patriarchal structures.
articles.imageDescription: A collage of book covers, one featuring a woman's face (for Salma's novel) and another showing street performers in action.
articles.trivia: Street theatre in India has a long history, often used during the independence movement and later for social awareness campaigns.

articles.headline: Boosting Domestic Manufacturing: Government Expands PLI Scheme to New Sectors
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government announced an expansion of the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme to include several new strategic sectors.
• This move aims to further enhance India's manufacturing capabilities, reduce import dependence, and create significant employment opportunities.
• The expanded scheme will focus on attracting global investments and promoting 'Make in India' initiatives in high-tech and value-added industries.
• Experts anticipate a positive impact on India's economic growth trajectory and global supply chain integration.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.

Major Crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, Different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.

Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
prelimsPoints: The PLI scheme was first introduced in March 2020 for three sectors and later expanded to 14 sectors.
The objective of PLI is to make Indian manufacturers globally competitive and create champions in various sectors.
mainsPoints: Analyze the objectives and mechanisms of the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme in boosting India's manufacturing sector.
Discuss the potential benefits and challenges associated with expanding the PLI scheme to new strategic sectors.
Evaluate the effectiveness of such incentive-based policies in achieving 'Make in India' goals and reducing import dependence.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic showing upward trending arrows over factory silhouettes, symbolizing growth in manufacturing.
articles.trivia: The PLI scheme is a key component of India's Atmanirbhar Bharat (Self-Reliant India) initiative.

articles.headline: India-EU Free Trade Agreement: Significant Progress Reported in Latest Round of Talks
articles.explainer:
• Negotiators from India and the European Union have concluded another round of talks for a comprehensive Free Trade Agreement (FTA), reporting significant progress.
• Key areas of discussion included market access for goods, services, investment, and intellectual property rights.
• A successful FTA would open vast economic opportunities for both sides, enhancing bilateral trade and investment flows.
• The agreement is expected to strengthen the India-EU strategic partnership amidst evolving global geopolitical landscapes.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India and the EU relaunched negotiations for a comprehensive FTA in 2021 after a hiatus.
The EU is one of India's largest trading partners, and India is a major market for EU exports.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of a Free Trade Agreement between India and the European Union.
Discuss the potential economic benefits and challenges for India arising from an FTA with the EU.
Examine how a strengthened India-EU economic partnership can contribute to global supply chain resilience and multilateralism.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and the European Union intertwined, symbolizing diplomatic and economic cooperation.
articles.trivia: The EU is a political and economic union of 27 member states located primarily in Europe.

articles.headline: Gaganyaan Mission Milestone: Critical Test Flight of Crew Escape System Successful
articles.explainer:
• ISRO successfully conducted a critical test flight for the Gaganyaan mission, focusing on the Crew Escape System (CES).
• The test validated the system's ability to safely abort the mission and extract astronauts in case of an anomaly during launch.
• This achievement marks a significant step forward in India's ambitious human spaceflight program.
• The Gaganyaan mission aims to send Indian astronauts into a low Earth orbit by 2025.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: The Gaganyaan mission aims to demonstrate India's capability to send humans to low Earth orbit.
The Crew Escape System (CES) is a crucial safety feature designed to quickly pull the crew module away from the launch vehicle in an emergency.
ISRO is the Indian Space Research Organisation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of India's human spaceflight program (Gaganyaan) for national prestige and technological advancement.
Explain the critical components and safety measures, such as the Crew Escape System, essential for manned space missions.
Analyze the broader implications of such missions for scientific research, international collaboration, and indigenous technological development.
articles.imageDescription: A rocket launching with a smaller module detaching, symbolizing the crew escape system test.
articles.trivia: The first Indian to travel to space was Rakesh Sharma in 1984, aboard a Soviet Soyuz T-11 mission.

articles.headline: Towards Cleaner Skies: Government Unveils Enhanced National Clean Air Programme Targets
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change announced enhanced targets and new strategies under the National Clean Air Programme (NCAP).
• The revised framework focuses on more stringent emission standards, increased monitoring, and public participation to combat urban air pollution.
• Special emphasis is placed on source apportionment studies and sector-specific interventions for industrial, vehicular, and construction emissions.
• This initiative underscores India's commitment to improving air quality and safeguarding public health in major cities.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: NCAP was launched in 2019 with the goal of reducing particulate matter concentration by 20-30% by 2024.
It is a long-term, time-bound, national-level strategy to tackle the air pollution problem across the country.
mainsPoints: Analyze the causes and impacts of urban air pollution in India, particularly in the context of public health and economic development.
Discuss the key features and enhanced targets of the National Clean Air Programme (NCAP).
Suggest comprehensive strategies, including technological, policy, and behavioral changes, to effectively combat air pollution in India.
articles.imageDescription: A cityscape with a clear blue sky contrasting with a previous image of smog, symbolizing improved air quality.
articles.trivia: Delhi often ranks among the most polluted cities globally, especially during the winter months.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Digital Rights: Landmark Ruling on Data Privacy and Surveillance
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India delivered a landmark judgment affirming fundamental digital rights, particularly concerning data privacy and state surveillance.
• The ruling emphasizes the necessity of a robust legal framework to protect citizens' digital footprints and prevent arbitrary data collection.
• It sets new precedents for balancing national security interests with individual privacy in the digital age.
• This decision is expected to have far-reaching implications for government agencies, tech companies, and data protection laws in India.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.
prelimsPoints: The Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared the Right to Privacy as a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution.
India is in the process of enacting a comprehensive data protection law, the Digital Personal Data Protection Act.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolution of the Right to Privacy in India's legal framework and its significance in the digital age.
Analyze the challenges of balancing national security concerns with individual data privacy rights, citing international best practices.
Evaluate the potential impact of this Supreme Court ruling on government policies, tech industry practices, and future data protection legislation in India.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel striking a sound block over a digital circuit board, symbolizing judicial intervention in technology.
articles.trivia: The first draft of India's data protection bill was proposed by the Justice B.N. Srikrishna Committee.

articles.headline: National Education Policy 2020: Focus on Skill Development and Vocational Integration
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education highlighted significant progress in implementing the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, with a renewed focus on skill development.
• Initiatives are underway to integrate vocational education into mainstream schooling from an early age, as envisioned by the NEP.
• The policy aims to equip students with practical skills, enhance employability, and align education with industry demands.
• This shift is crucial for India's demographic dividend and fostering a knowledge-based economy.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 replaced the 1986 National Policy on Education.
One of the key recommendations of NEP 2020 is to integrate vocational education into all school and higher education institutions.
mainsPoints: Discuss the salient features of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, with a focus on its provisions for skill development.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in integrating vocational education into the mainstream Indian education system.
Evaluate how NEP 2020's emphasis on skill development can contribute to India's economic growth and address the issue of youth unemployment.
articles.imageDescription: Students in a workshop setting, learning practical skills, with textbooks in the background.
articles.trivia: India has one of the youngest populations in the world, making skill development crucial for its economic future.

articles.headline: Integrated Border Management System Bolstered Along Western Front
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Home Affairs announced the successful deployment of an enhanced Integrated Border Management System (IBMS) along critical stretches of the Western Front.
• This advanced system incorporates cutting-edge surveillance technology, including drones, sensors, and artificial intelligence for real-time monitoring.
• The move aims to significantly curb infiltration, smuggling, and cross-border terrorism activities.
• This strategic upgrade is part of India's ongoing efforts to modernize border security infrastructure and enhance national security.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
prelimsPoints: The IBMS integrates various technologies like radars, sensors, cameras, and communication systems for comprehensive border monitoring.
The Border Security Force (BSF) is primarily responsible for guarding India's land borders during peacetime.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted challenges faced by India in managing its land borders, particularly along the Western Front.
Explain the concept and technological components of an Integrated Border Management System (IBMS) and its advantages.
Critically evaluate the effectiveness of technological solutions in combating cross-border infiltration and terrorism, alongside human intelligence and diplomatic efforts.
articles.imageDescription: A drone flying over a border fence equipped with sensors, with a control room display in the background.
articles.trivia: India shares a 3,323 km long border with Pakistan, known as the International Border (IB) and Line of Control (LoC).

articles.headline: India's UPI Model: Global Interest Grows for Digital Public Infrastructure Adoption
articles.explainer:
• India's Unified Payments Interface (UPI) continues to garner international attention, with several countries exploring its adoption for their own digital payment systems.
• The success of UPI as a robust and inclusive digital public infrastructure (DPI) is being studied as a model for financial inclusion and cashless transactions.
• Delegations from various nations have visited India to understand the technical architecture and regulatory framework of UPI.
• This growing global interest positions India as a leader in digital innovation and financial technology.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: UPI was launched in 2016 by the National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI).
It allows instant real-time payments through a mobile application.
Countries like France, Singapore, UAE, and Bhutan have already partnered with India for UPI or similar payment systems.
mainsPoints: Explain the functioning and key features of India's Unified Payments Interface (UPI) as a Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI).
Analyze the factors contributing to UPI's success in India and its potential for fostering financial inclusion globally.
Discuss the implications of India's leadership in digital payments for its soft power and economic diplomacy.
articles.imageDescription: A globe with various national flags connected to a central digital payment icon, symbolizing global UPI adoption.
articles.trivia: UPI processed over 10 billion transactions in a single month for the first time in August 2023.

articles.headline: Strengthening Grassroots Governance: Renewed Focus on Empowering Panchayati Raj Institutions
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Panchayati Raj announced new initiatives aimed at further empowering Panchayati Raj Institutions (PRIs) across the country.
• The focus is on enhancing financial autonomy, capacity building of elected representatives, and digitalizing local governance processes.
• These reforms seek to strengthen decentralization and ensure more effective delivery of public services at the village level.
• Empowering PRIs is crucial for achieving inclusive rural development and deepening democratic participation.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.
prelimsPoints: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act (1992) gave constitutional status to Panchayati Raj Institutions.
It mandated the establishment of a three-tier system of Panchayats at the village, intermediate, and district levels.
mainsPoints: Discuss the constitutional provisions and significance of Panchayati Raj Institutions in India's democratic framework.
Analyze the major challenges faced by PRIs in effective functioning, including financial constraints and capacity gaps.
Suggest reforms and strategies to further empower PRIs for inclusive rural development and enhanced grassroots governance.
articles.imageDescription: A hand holding a sapling in a rural setting, symbolizing growth and development at the grassroots level.
articles.trivia: Rajasthan was the first state to implement Panchayati Raj in 1959.

articles.headline: Revitalizing India's Traditional Crafts: New Government Schemes for Artisan Livelihoods
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Culture launched a series of new schemes aimed at revitalizing India's rich heritage of traditional crafts and supporting artisan livelihoods.
• Initiatives include skill upgradation, market linkage programs, e-commerce integration, and intellectual property protection for traditional designs.
• The objective is to preserve indigenous art forms, boost rural economies, and promote India's cultural identity globally.
• This move will empower countless artisans, particularly women and marginalized communities, by providing sustainable income opportunities.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Social empowerment
prelimsPoints: India is home to a vast array of traditional crafts, many of which are region-specific and passed down through generations.
Schemes like 'Virasat' and 'PM Vishwakarma Kaushal Samman' (PM VIKAS) aim to support traditional artisans.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of traditional Indian crafts for the nation's cultural heritage and rural economy.
Analyze the challenges faced by traditional artisans in the contemporary market, including competition and lack of market access.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government initiatives in preserving traditional crafts and ensuring sustainable livelihoods for artisans.
articles.imageDescription: A close-up of intricate handwoven fabric or pottery, showcasing the skill of traditional artisans.
articles.trivia: Many Indian crafts have received Geographical Indication (GI) tags, signifying their unique origin and qualities.

articles.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission: Progress in Creating a Unified Health ID System
articles.explainer:
• The National Health Authority (NHA) reported significant progress in the implementation of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM).
• The mission aims to create a seamless online platform for interoperable digital health infrastructure, including a unique health ID for every citizen.
• This digital ecosystem will facilitate access to health records, teleconsultations, and paperless healthcare services.
• ABDM is a transformative step towards universal health coverage and a more efficient healthcare system in India.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: ABDM was launched in September 2021 by the Prime Minister.
It aims to create a digital highway for healthcare by building a comprehensive digital health ecosystem.
The Health ID is a 14-digit number that uniquely identifies an individual within the digital health system.
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and key components of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) in transforming India's healthcare landscape.
Analyze the potential benefits and challenges of creating a unified digital health ID system for citizens.
Evaluate how digital health initiatives like ABDM can contribute to achieving Universal Health Coverage and improving public health outcomes in India.
articles.imageDescription: A network of interconnected digital health icons, with a central 'Health ID' card graphic.
articles.trivia: The Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB PM-JAY) is the world's largest government-funded health insurance scheme.

highlights: Historian Tanika Sarkar emphasizes the silent, deep societal penetration and pervasive influence of the RSS, making its reach hard to gauge.
The crucial ideological distinction between Hindutva and Hinduism is debated, highlighting Hindutva's exclusionary nature.
India-EU Free Trade Agreement negotiations show significant progress, promising vast economic opportunities and stronger strategic partnership.
ISRO successfully conducts a critical test flight for the Gaganyaan mission's Crew Escape System, a major step for human spaceflight.
The Supreme Court delivers a landmark judgment upholding fundamental digital rights, emphasizing data privacy and regulating state surveillance.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme, consider the following statements:
1. The scheme was launched in 2020 to boost domestic manufacturing in select sectors.
2. It aims to reduce import dependence and make Indian manufacturers globally competitive.
3. The scheme is currently applicable to only three core sectors.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The PLI scheme was indeed launched in March 2020. Statement 2 is correct: Its primary objectives are to enhance manufacturing, reduce imports, and boost global competitiveness. Statement 3 is incorrect: While it started with three sectors, it has since been expanded to 14 sectors and is further expanding, as mentioned in the article.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following statements best describes the concept of 'Akhand Bharat'?
options: (a) A unified economic zone comprising India and South-East Asian nations.
(b) A historical ideal of an undivided Indian subcontinent including present-day India, Pakistan, Bangladesh, and surrounding regions.
(c) A proposal for a military alliance between India, Nepal, and Bhutan.
(d) A cultural exchange program promoting Indian arts and traditions globally.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) A historical ideal of an undivided Indian subcontinent including present-day India, Pakistan, Bangladesh, and surrounding regions.
prelimsMocks.explanation: Akhand Bharat is a historical and ideological concept referring to a greater India, encompassing the entire Indian subcontinent and often extending to neighbouring regions like Afghanistan, Nepal, Bhutan, Tibet, and Myanmar, implying a unified territory before various historical partitions.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following about the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM):
1. It aims to create a seamless online platform for interoperable digital health infrastructure.
2. A unique 14-digit Health ID is provided to every citizen under this mission.
3. The mission focuses exclusively on providing free health insurance to all citizens.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statements 1 and 2 are correct. ABDM aims to build a digital health ecosystem and provides a unique Health ID. Statement 3 is incorrect. While Ayushman Bharat includes a health insurance scheme (PM-JAY), the Digital Mission (ABDM) focuses on digital infrastructure and health records, not directly on providing health insurance.

prelimsMocks.question: The term 'Hindutva', often discussed in contemporary Indian politics, was primarily articulated by:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Vinayak Damodar Savarkar is widely credited with articulating the concept of Hindutva in his 1923 book 'Essentials of Hindutva', which laid down the ideological foundation for Hindu nationalism.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the National Clean Air Programme (NCAP), which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. It is a long-term, time-bound, national-level strategy to tackle air pollution.
2. It aims to reduce particulate matter concentration by 20-30% by 2024 (from 2017 levels).
3. It focuses only on industrial emissions, excluding vehicular pollution.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: NCAP is indeed a long-term, national strategy. Statement 2 is correct: The initial target was to reduce PM concentration by 20-30% by 2024. Statement 3 is incorrect: NCAP addresses multiple sources of pollution, including industrial, vehicular, and construction emissions, among others.

mainsMocks.question: Critically analyze the assertion that 'Hindutva is not Hinduism'. Discuss the implications of conflating a political ideology with a diverse religion for India's secular fabric and social harmony. (15 marks, 250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure; Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
mainsMocks.approach: Define both Hindutva (political ideology, V.D. Savarkar) and Hinduism (diverse, ancient religion). Highlight key differences: Hindutva's emphasis on cultural nationalism, territoriality, and often exclusionary elements vs. Hinduism's pluralism, philosophical diversity, and lack of a single founder/text. Discuss the implications: erosion of secularism, communal polarization, marginalization of minorities, impact on constitutional values. Conclude with the importance of maintaining this distinction for India's democratic and pluralistic identity.

mainsMocks.question: India's progress is intricately linked to fostering Hindu–Muslim harmony. In this context, evaluate the role and potential for organizations like the RSS to shed exclusionary ideas and contribute to social healing and national unity. (15 marks, 250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS1: Social empowerment, communalism, regionalism, secularism; GS2: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
mainsMocks.approach: Establish the premise that Hindu-Muslim harmony is vital for India's progress. Briefly discuss the historical and ideological positions of the RSS, particularly concerning its perceived exclusionary ideas. Analyze the challenges for such an organization to change its core tenets. Explore potential pathways for contribution to harmony: focus on common cultural heritage, community service, dialogue, and shedding divisive rhetoric. Conclude by assessing the responsibility of all societal actors, including powerful organizations, in building an inclusive India.

mainsMocks.question: The success of India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) like UPI has garnered global interest. Discuss the factors contributing to UPI's success and analyze its potential impact on global financial inclusion and India's soft power. (15 marks, 250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment; Inclusive growth and issues arising from it; Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce UPI as a successful DPI. Discuss factors for its success: real-time payments, interoperability, ease of use, low cost, robust security, government backing, API-driven architecture, and widespread adoption. Analyze its impact on global financial inclusion: providing a model for other developing nations, reducing transaction costs, enabling digital economy. Explain how it enhances India's soft power through technological leadership, demonstrating a scalable and inclusive digital solution. Conclude with the broader implications for global digital governance.

quickBytes.text: The RSS was founded in 1925 by Keshav Baliram Hedgewar.
Vinayak Damodar Savarkar articulated the concept of 'Hindutva' in his 1923 book.
The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act (1992) gave constitutional status to Panchayati Raj Institutions.
ISRO's Gaganyaan mission aims to send Indian astronauts into a low Earth orbit by 2025.
The National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 replaced the 1986 National Policy on Education.
India's UPI processed over 10 billion transactions in a single month for the first time in August 2023.
The Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared the Right to Privacy as a fundamental right.
NCAP (National Clean Air Programme) was launched in 2019 to tackle air pollution.
The EU is one of India's largest trading partners.
Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) was launched in September 2021.

GS1.headline: RSS's Silent Infiltration: Historian Tanika Sarkar on its Deep Societal Reach
GS1.explainer:
• Renowned historian Tanika Sarkar highlights the silent, pervasive influence of the Rashtriya Swayamsevak Sangh (RSS) across Indian society.
• The RSS's methodical approach makes its organizational depth and reach difficult for external observers to fully gauge.
• This strategy allows the organization to embed its ideology at various grassroots levels without overt visibility.
• Concerns are raised about the long-term implications of such widespread, subtle ideological penetration on national discourse.
gsSubTopics: Role of women and women’s organization
population and associated issues
poverty and developmental issues
urbanization, their problems and their remedies
effects of globalization on Indian society
social empowerment
communalism
regionalism
secularism
prelimsPoints: Tanika Sarkar is a prominent Indian historian known for her work on gender, religion, and nationalism.
RSS is a right-wing Hindu nationalist paramilitary volunteer organization in India.
mainsPoints: Analyze the methods employed by organizations like RSS to achieve deep societal penetration.
Discuss the challenges in assessing the true extent and impact of such 'silent' organizational work.
Critically evaluate the implications of widespread ideological influence on India's secular fabric and democratic institutions.
GS1.imageDescription: A blurred image depicting a large gathering, symbolizing widespread societal presence, with a subtle saffron hue.
GS1.trivia: The RSS was founded in 1925 by Keshav Baliram Hedgewar.

GS1.headline: The Concept of Akhand Bharat: Historical Ideal or Modern Political Tool?
GS1.explainer:
• The idea of 'Akhand Bharat' (Undivided India) is explored, questioning its feasibility and relevance in contemporary geopolitics.
• Historically, Akhand Bharat refers to a unified Indian subcontinent encompassing present-day India, Pakistan, Bangladesh, Afghanistan, Nepal, Bhutan, Tibet, and Myanmar.
• The article delves into whether this historical ideal can be 'reimagined' in the 21st century or if it serves primarily as a nationalist narrative.
• It examines the challenges and implications of such a concept for regional stability and international relations.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Modern Indian history from about the middle of the eighteenth century until the present- significant events, personalities, issues.
The Freedom Struggle — its various stages and important contributors /contributions from different parts of the country.
Post-independence consolidation and reorganization within the country.
prelimsPoints: The concept of Akhand Bharat is often associated with the Hindu nationalist movement.
The idea envisions a greater India that includes territories historically or culturally linked to the Indian subcontinent.
mainsPoints: Discuss the historical origins and evolution of the concept of Akhand Bharat.
Analyze the political and cultural motivations behind advocating for Akhand Bharat in modern India.
Evaluate the geopolitical implications and challenges of reimagining Akhand Bharat in the current international order.
GS1.imageDescription: An old map of the Indian subcontinent showing its historical territorial extent, overlaid with modern borders.
GS1.trivia: The geographical extent of Akhand Bharat has varied in different historical and ideological interpretations.

GS1.headline: Literary Landscape: New Books on Feminist Street Theatre and Salma's Translated Novel
GS1.explainer:
• The latest literary releases highlight a fresh translation of Tamil writer Salma’s debut novel, bringing her work to a wider audience.
• Another notable book offers a richly detailed study of feminist street theatre in India, exploring its role in social commentary and activism.
• These publications underscore the vibrant and diverse literary and cultural scene in India, focusing on critical social themes.
• The books contribute to understanding gender dynamics, cultural expression, and regional literature.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Role of women and women’s organization
social empowerment
prelimsPoints: Salma is a prominent contemporary Tamil writer, poet, and activist.
Feminist street theatre often employs nukkad natak (street play) format to raise awareness on social issues.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of regional literature translations in promoting cultural exchange and understanding across India.
Analyze the role of feminist street theatre as a tool for social change and empowerment in India.
Examine how contemporary literature addresses critical social issues like gender inequality and patriarchal structures.
GS1.imageDescription: A collage of book covers, one featuring a woman's face (for Salma's novel) and another showing street performers in action.
GS1.trivia: Street theatre in India has a long history, often used during the independence movement and later for social awareness campaigns.

GS1.headline: Revitalizing India's Traditional Crafts: New Government Schemes for Artisan Livelihoods
GS1.explainer:
• The Ministry of Culture launched a series of new schemes aimed at revitalizing India's rich heritage of traditional crafts and supporting artisan livelihoods.
• Initiatives include skill upgradation, market linkage programs, e-commerce integration, and intellectual property protection for traditional designs.
• The objective is to preserve indigenous art forms, boost rural economies, and promote India's cultural identity globally.
• This move will empower countless artisans, particularly women and marginalized communities, by providing sustainable income opportunities.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Social empowerment
prelimsPoints: India is home to a vast array of traditional crafts, many of which are region-specific and passed down through generations.
Schemes like 'Virasat' and 'PM Vishwakarma Kaushal Samman' (PM VIKAS) aim to support traditional artisans.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of traditional Indian crafts for the nation's cultural heritage and rural economy.
Analyze the challenges faced by traditional artisans in the contemporary market, including competition and lack of market access.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government initiatives in preserving traditional crafts and ensuring sustainable livelihoods for artisans.
GS1.imageDescription: A close-up of intricate handwoven fabric or pottery, showcasing the skill of traditional artisans.
GS1.trivia: Many Indian crafts have received Geographical Indication (GI) tags, signifying their unique origin and qualities.

GS2.headline: Distinguishing Ideology from Religion: Why 'Hindutva is Not Hinduism' Remains a Core Debate
GS2.explainer:
• The article reiterates the crucial distinction between Hindutva as a political ideology and Hinduism as a diverse religion.
• Scholars argue that Hindutva, often characterized by its exclusionary principles, cannot be equated with the inclusive and pluralistic tenets of Hinduism.
• The assertion 'No Muslim, no Hindutva' is analyzed to highlight the inherent exclusionary nature of the Hindutva ideology.
• This conceptual clarity is vital for understanding contemporary political and social discourse in India.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.
prelimsPoints: Hindutva was primarily articulated by Vinayak Damodar Savarkar in his 1923 book 'Essentials of Hindutva'.
The Supreme Court of India has, in various judgments, distinguished between Hindutva as a way of life and a political ideology.
mainsPoints: Elaborate on the historical and ideological differences between Hindutva and Hinduism.
Discuss the implications of conflating a political ideology with a religion for India's constitutional secularism.
Analyze how the 'No Muslim, no Hindutva' perspective shapes political narratives and societal divisions in India.
GS2.imageDescription: A conceptual image showing two distinct thought bubbles, one representing diverse religious symbols and the other a rigid, singular political symbol.

GS2.headline: Fostering Harmony: Can RSS Shed Exclusionary Ideas for India's Progress?
GS2.explainer:
• Sudheendra Kulkarni argues that India's progress is intrinsically linked to fostering Hindu–Muslim harmony and healing historical wounds.
• The article critically examines whether the RSS, known for its exclusionary ideas, possesses the imagination to adapt and embrace inclusivity.
• It posits a challenge to the RSS to move beyond its current ideological framework for the greater good of national unity.
• The debate centers on whether a major societal organization can evolve its core tenets to align with a pluralistic vision of India.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of communal harmony as a prerequisite for national progress and development in India.
Critically analyze the historical stance of the RSS on Hindu-Muslim relations and its implications for national integration.
Suggest pathways through which organizations like the RSS could contribute to fostering an inclusive and harmonious society in India.
GS2.imageDescription: An illustration showing hands of different faiths clasped together, symbolizing unity and harmony.
GS2.trivia: Mahatma Gandhi strongly advocated for Hindu-Muslim unity as essential for India's independence and future.

GS2.headline: Internal Power Dynamics: Will RSS Maintain Control Over its Most Successful Political Creation?
GS2.explainer:
• The article probes the evolving relationship between the RSS and its political offshoots, particularly the ruling party.
• It raises questions about the extent to which the RSS continues to exert ideological and organizational control over its 'most successful creation'.
• Speculation suggests potential shifts in power dynamics, where the political arm might assert greater autonomy.
• This dynamic is crucial for understanding future policy directions and the ideological trajectory of the larger nationalist movement.
gsSubTopics: Pressure Groups and Formal/Informal Associations and their role in the Polity.
prelimsPoints: The Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) is widely considered the political offshoot of the RSS.
The RSS provides a significant cadre base and ideological guidance to various organizations within the Sangh Parivar.
mainsPoints: Analyze the historical relationship between the RSS and its political wings, outlining the nature of control and influence.
Discuss the factors that could lead to a potential shift in the power dynamic between the RSS and its political creation.
Evaluate the implications of such a shift for Indian politics and governance.
GS2.imageDescription: An intricate diagram showing a central hub (RSS) connected to multiple spokes (political/social organizations), with some connections appearing stressed.
GS2.trivia: The first Prime Minister from the BJP, Atal Bihari Vajpayee, was a lifelong member of the RSS.

GS2.headline: India-EU Free Trade Agreement: Significant Progress Reported in Latest Round of Talks
GS2.explainer:
• Negotiators from India and the European Union have concluded another round of talks for a comprehensive Free Trade Agreement (FTA), reporting significant progress.
• Key areas of discussion included market access for goods, services, investment, and intellectual property rights.
• A successful FTA would open vast economic opportunities for both sides, enhancing bilateral trade and investment flows.
• The agreement is expected to strengthen the India-EU strategic partnership amidst evolving global geopolitical landscapes.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India and the EU relaunched negotiations for a comprehensive FTA in 2021 after a hiatus.
The EU is one of India's largest trading partners, and India is a major market for EU exports.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of a Free Trade Agreement between India and the European Union.
Discuss the potential economic benefits and challenges for India arising from an FTA with the EU.
Examine how a strengthened India-EU economic partnership can contribute to global supply chain resilience and multilateralism.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and the European Union intertwined, symbolizing diplomatic and economic cooperation.
GS2.trivia: The EU is a political and economic union of 27 member states located primarily in Europe.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Digital Rights: Landmark Ruling on Data Privacy and Surveillance
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India delivered a landmark judgment affirming fundamental digital rights, particularly concerning data privacy and state surveillance.
• The ruling emphasizes the necessity of a robust legal framework to protect citizens' digital footprints and prevent arbitrary data collection.
• It sets new precedents for balancing national security interests with individual privacy in the digital age.
• This decision is expected to have far-reaching implications for government agencies, tech companies, and data protection laws in India.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.
prelimsPoints: The Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared the Right to Privacy as a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution.
India is in the process of enacting a comprehensive data protection law, the Digital Personal Data Protection Act.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolution of the Right to Privacy in India's legal framework and its significance in the digital age.
Analyze the challenges of balancing national security concerns with individual data privacy rights, citing international best practices.
Evaluate the potential impact of this Supreme Court ruling on government policies, tech industry practices, and future data protection legislation in India.
GS2.imageDescription: A gavel striking a sound block over a digital circuit board, symbolizing judicial intervention in technology.
GS2.trivia: The first draft of India's data protection bill was proposed by the Justice B.N. Srikrishna Committee.

GS2.headline: National Education Policy 2020: Focus on Skill Development and Vocational Integration
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education highlighted significant progress in implementing the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, with a renewed focus on skill development.
• Initiatives are underway to integrate vocational education into mainstream schooling from an early age, as envisioned by the NEP.
• The policy aims to equip students with practical skills, enhance employability, and align education with industry demands.
• This shift is crucial for India's demographic dividend and fostering a knowledge-based economy.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 replaced the 1986 National Policy on Education.
One of the key recommendations of NEP 2020 is to integrate vocational education into all school and higher education institutions.
mainsPoints: Discuss the salient features of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, with a focus on its provisions for skill development.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in integrating vocational education into the mainstream Indian education system.
Evaluate how NEP 2020's emphasis on skill development can contribute to India's economic growth and address the issue of youth unemployment.
GS2.imageDescription: Students in a workshop setting, learning practical skills, with textbooks in the background.
GS2.trivia: India has one of the youngest populations in the world, making skill development crucial for its economic future.

GS2.headline: Strengthening Grassroots Governance: Renewed Focus on Empowering Panchayati Raj Institutions
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Panchayati Raj announced new initiatives aimed at further empowering Panchayati Raj Institutions (PRIs) across the country.
• The focus is on enhancing financial autonomy, capacity building of elected representatives, and digitalizing local governance processes.
• These reforms seek to strengthen decentralization and ensure more effective delivery of public services at the village level.
• Empowering PRIs is crucial for achieving inclusive rural development and deepening democratic participation.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.
prelimsPoints: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act (1992) gave constitutional status to Panchayati Raj Institutions.
It mandated the establishment of a three-tier system of Panchayats at the village, intermediate, and district levels.
mainsPoints: Discuss the constitutional provisions and significance of Panchayati Raj Institutions in India's democratic framework.
Analyze the major challenges faced by PRIs in effective functioning, including financial constraints and capacity gaps.
Suggest reforms and strategies to further empower PRIs for inclusive rural development and enhanced grassroots governance.
GS2.imageDescription: A hand holding a sapling in a rural setting, symbolizing growth and development at the grassroots level.
GS2.trivia: Rajasthan was the first state to implement Panchayati Raj in 1959.

GS2.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission: Progress in Creating a Unified Health ID System
GS2.explainer:
• The National Health Authority (NHA) reported significant progress in the implementation of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM).
• The mission aims to create a seamless online platform for interoperable digital health infrastructure, including a unique health ID for every citizen.
• This digital ecosystem will facilitate access to health records, teleconsultations, and paperless healthcare services.
• ABDM is a transformative step towards universal health coverage and a more efficient healthcare system in India.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: ABDM was launched in September 2021 by the Prime Minister.
It aims to create a digital highway for healthcare by building a comprehensive digital health ecosystem.
The Health ID is a 14-digit number that uniquely identifies an individual within the digital health system.
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and key components of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) in transforming India's healthcare landscape.
Analyze the potential benefits and challenges of creating a unified digital health ID system for citizens.
Evaluate how digital health initiatives like ABDM can contribute to achieving Universal Health Coverage and improving public health outcomes in India.
GS2.imageDescription: A network of interconnected digital health icons, with a central 'Health ID' card graphic.
GS2.trivia: The Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB PM-JAY) is the world's largest government-funded health insurance scheme.

GS3.headline: Boosting Domestic Manufacturing: Government Expands PLI Scheme to New Sectors
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Government announced an expansion of the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme to include several new strategic sectors.
• This move aims to further enhance India's manufacturing capabilities, reduce import dependence, and create significant employment opportunities.
• The expanded scheme will focus on attracting global investments and promoting 'Make in India' initiatives in high-tech and value-added industries.
• Experts anticipate a positive impact on India's economic growth trajectory and global supply chain integration.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.

Major Crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, Different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.

Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
prelimsPoints: The PLI scheme was first introduced in March 2020 for three sectors and later expanded to 14 sectors.
The objective of PLI is to make Indian manufacturers globally competitive and create champions in various sectors.
mainsPoints: Analyze the objectives and mechanisms of the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme in boosting India's manufacturing sector.
Discuss the potential benefits and challenges associated with expanding the PLI scheme to new strategic sectors.
Evaluate the effectiveness of such incentive-based policies in achieving 'Make in India' goals and reducing import dependence.
GS3.imageDescription: A graphic showing upward trending arrows over factory silhouettes, symbolizing growth in manufacturing.
GS3.trivia: The PLI scheme is a key component of India's Atmanirbhar Bharat (Self-Reliant India) initiative.

GS3.headline: Gaganyaan Mission Milestone: Critical Test Flight of Crew Escape System Successful
GS3.explainer:
• ISRO successfully conducted a critical test flight for the Gaganyaan mission, focusing on the Crew Escape System (CES).
• The test validated the system's ability to safely abort the mission and extract astronauts in case of an anomaly during launch.
• This achievement marks a significant step forward in India's ambitious human spaceflight program.
• The Gaganyaan mission aims to send Indian astronauts into a low Earth orbit by 2025.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: The Gaganyaan mission aims to demonstrate India's capability to send humans to low Earth orbit.
The Crew Escape System (CES) is a crucial safety feature designed to quickly pull the crew module away from the launch vehicle in an emergency.
ISRO is the Indian Space Research Organisation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of India's human spaceflight program (Gaganyaan) for national prestige and technological advancement.
Explain the critical components and safety measures, such as the Crew Escape System, essential for manned space missions.
Analyze the broader implications of such missions for scientific research, international collaboration, and indigenous technological development.
GS3.imageDescription: A rocket launching with a smaller module detaching, symbolizing the crew escape system test.
GS3.trivia: The first Indian to travel to space was Rakesh Sharma in 1984, aboard a Soviet Soyuz T-11 mission.

GS3.headline: Towards Cleaner Skies: Government Unveils Enhanced National Clean Air Programme Targets
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change announced enhanced targets and new strategies under the National Clean Air Programme (NCAP).
• The revised framework focuses on more stringent emission standards, increased monitoring, and public participation to combat urban air pollution.
• Special emphasis is placed on source apportionment studies and sector-specific interventions for industrial, vehicular, and construction emissions.
• This initiative underscores India's commitment to improving air quality and safeguarding public health in major cities.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
GS3.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: NCAP was launched in 2019 with the goal of reducing particulate matter concentration by 20-30% by 2024.
It is a long-term, time-bound, national-level strategy to tackle the air pollution problem across the country.
mainsPoints: Analyze the causes and impacts of urban air pollution in India, particularly in the context of public health and economic development.
Discuss the key features and enhanced targets of the National Clean Air Programme (NCAP).
Suggest comprehensive strategies, including technological, policy, and behavioral changes, to effectively combat air pollution in India.
GS3.imageDescription: A cityscape with a clear blue sky contrasting with a previous image of smog, symbolizing improved air quality.
GS3.trivia: Delhi often ranks among the most polluted cities globally, especially during the winter months.

GS3.headline: Integrated Border Management System Bolstered Along Western Front
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Home Affairs announced the successful deployment of an enhanced Integrated Border Management System (IBMS) along critical stretches of the Western Front.
• This advanced system incorporates cutting-edge surveillance technology, including drones, sensors, and artificial intelligence for real-time monitoring.
• The move aims to significantly curb infiltration, smuggling, and cross-border terrorism activities.
• This strategic upgrade is part of India's ongoing efforts to modernize border security infrastructure and enhance national security.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
prelimsPoints: The IBMS integrates various technologies like radars, sensors, cameras, and communication systems for comprehensive border monitoring.
The Border Security Force (BSF) is primarily responsible for guarding India's land borders during peacetime.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted challenges faced by India in managing its land borders, particularly along the Western Front.
Explain the concept and technological components of an Integrated Border Management System (IBMS) and its advantages.
Critically evaluate the effectiveness of technological solutions in combating cross-border infiltration and terrorism, alongside human intelligence and diplomatic efforts.
GS3.imageDescription: A drone flying over a border fence equipped with sensors, with a control room display in the background.
GS3.trivia: India shares a 3,323 km long border with Pakistan, known as the International Border (IB) and Line of Control (LoC).

GS3.headline: India's UPI Model: Global Interest Grows for Digital Public Infrastructure Adoption
GS3.explainer:
• India's Unified Payments Interface (UPI) continues to garner international attention, with several countries exploring its adoption for their own digital payment systems.
• The success of UPI as a robust and inclusive digital public infrastructure (DPI) is being studied as a model for financial inclusion and cashless transactions.
• Delegations from various nations have visited India to understand the technical architecture and regulatory framework of UPI.
• This growing global interest positions India as a leader in digital innovation and financial technology.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: UPI was launched in 2016 by the National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI).
It allows instant real-time payments through a mobile application.
Countries like France, Singapore, UAE, and Bhutan have already partnered with India for UPI or similar payment systems.
mainsPoints: Explain the functioning and key features of India's Unified Payments Interface (UPI) as a Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI).
Analyze the factors contributing to UPI's success in India and its potential for fostering financial inclusion globally.
Discuss the implications of India's leadership in digital payments for its soft power and economic diplomacy.
GS3.imageDescription: A globe with various national flags connected to a central digital payment icon, symbolizing global UPI adoption.
GS3.trivia: UPI processed over 10 billion transactions in a single month for the first time in August 2023.

sources: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
